note: Extracted directly from the live site (data-id / data-tip attributes). Cell ids are the site's own stable ids. links.predecessors/successors are empty pending extraction of the relation map; how.* fields are reserved for the future HOW dimension.

cellSchema.label: cell title
cellSchema.tooltip: hover detail (plain text - sanitize on render)

tactical.label: Competitive Research
tactical.tooltip: Know who your competitors are targeting; know their pricing; know their exam structure.

tactical.label: Audience Description
tactical.tooltip: Define the target audience persona for the certification.

tactical.label: Demand / Gap Analysis
tactical.tooltip: Identify gaps in market skill sets vs. what the certification validates.

tactical.label: Candidate Segmentation
tactical.tooltip: Splitting the eligible market into prioritized groups by motivation, demographics, and access.

tactical.label: Addressable Market Sizing
tactical.tooltip: Numeric estimate of eligible candidate population, growth rate, and realistic conversion. Similar to SAM (Serviceable Addressable Market)

tactical.label: Reqs. & Titles
tactical.tooltip: Create the naming convention and title structure for the certification track.

tactical.label: Testing & Scoring Goals
tactical.tooltip: Establish high-level targets for candidate volume, pass rates, and revenue contribution.

tactical.label: Program Positioning
tactical.tooltip: Create a program positioning statement - how the cert is differentiated in the market.

strategic.label: Audience Analysis
strategic.tooltip: Deep dive of who takes the cert, why they take it and what they do with it after taking it.

strategic.label: Employer Demand Analysis
strategic.tooltip: Why employers require/incentivize the certification; conditions for employer-mandated adoption.

strategic.label: Partner Demand Analysis
strategic.tooltip: How to measure partner program candidate demand.

strategic.label: Competitive Differentiation Strategy
strategic.tooltip: How the program will differentiate (brand/prestige vs. feature vs. niche focus).

strategic.label: Environmental Scan
strategic.tooltip: PESTLE analysis; Political, Economic, Sociological, Technological, Legal and Environmental forces affecting the program.

strategic.label: Credential Value Proposition
strategic.tooltip: Tangible and intangible benefits the credential delivers to holders, which drives all positioning.

strategic.label: Market Needs Analysis
strategic.tooltip: Market research demonstrating demand: skills shortages, industry trends, and job posting data.

strategic.label: Industry Awareness
strategic.tooltip: Knowledge of industry bodies, standards, and certification landscape changes.

strategic.label: Job Role Awareness
strategic.tooltip: Understanding of the job roles the certification targets and how those jobs are changing.

strategic.label: Business Drivers
strategic.tooltip: What business outcomes does the certification program serve? Revenue, pipeline, product adoption?

strategic.label: Funding Model / Pricing
strategic.tooltip: How will the program be funded? Exam fees, training packages, partner funding? What price tiers and discounts will be offered

tactical.label: Stage Gate Checklists
tactical.tooltip: Stage Gate Checklists - what must be done before advancing from one stage of the certification process to the next (JTA to Item Writing, Beta to Live, etc.)

tactical.label: Risk & Issue Management
tactical.tooltip: Risk and Issue Log for Active Exam Development Projects - escalation procedures established.

tactical.label: Vendor Management
tactical.tooltip: Management of vendors and psychometricians - service level agreements (SLAs), deliverables, contract compliance.

tactical.label: Tech Stack Integration Management
tactical.tooltip: Keeping the certification systems connected and functioning: item bank, delivery platform, badging, credentialing, and candidate database data flows.

tactical.label: Decision Log
tactical.tooltip: Documentation of decisions made during the certification development process for auditing purposes and institutional knowledge.

tactical.label: Process & Template Library
tactical.tooltip: Templates for JTA, Blueprints, Item Writing and Standard Setting to ensure consistency across programs.

tactical.label: Program Budget & Cost Model
tactical.tooltip: Line item budget for SME travel and expense, item banking tools, delivery platforms, digital badging provider, certificate tracking system (different from Learning Management System), marketing/web, and operations personnel. Review and update annually.

strategic.label: Decision Rights & RACI
strategic.tooltip: Define how decisions are made - who makes decisions regarding new tracks, exam updates, pricing changes and retirement. RACI across departments.

strategic.label: Governance Structure & Cadence
strategic.tooltip: Frequency and format of cross-functional steering, quarterly program reviews, annual strategy sessions, and launch approvals. Without documented cadence, strategic decisions get made reactively and accountability gaps emerge.

strategic.label: Tech Stack Architecture
strategic.tooltip: Defining the full systems landscape: which platforms are used, how they integrate, data ownership, and vendor dependency strategy.

strategic.label: Policy Framework
strategic.tooltip: Define what constitutes a policy change versus process update versus tactical decision. Create a 'living policy handbook'.

strategic.label: Continuous Improvement Loop
strategic.tooltip: Continuous improvement loop - How learnings from Health Checks, Analytics, and Candidate Feedback influence program strategy.

strategic.label: Program Review Workshop
strategic.tooltip: Create a formal periodic stakeholder workshop - review full certification program health checklist - cross functionally, pressure test roadmap and define next cycle priorities. Different from routine steering meetings.

strategic.label: Accreditation Strategy
strategic.tooltip: Deciding whether to pursue third-party accreditation, the timeline, and the resource commitment required.

tactical.label: Track Map / Plan
tactical.tooltip: Visual representation of the certification path - levels, prerequisites, specialty tracks.

tactical.label: Monitor Release Schedule
tactical.tooltip: When each exam within the track will be released - scheduling and sequencing.

tactical.label: Align SME Resources
tactical.tooltip: Who are the subject matter experts (SMEs) best suited for each track - identify and align.

tactical.label: Pricing Execution & Vouchers
tactical.tooltip: Candidate volume discounts programs, retake policies, voucher management, and reconciling revenue - manage.

strategic.label: SME/Partner Interviews
strategic.tooltip: Gather input from partner organizations and SMEs - track design, relevance, and content.

strategic.label: Audience Surveys
strategic.tooltip: Gather candidate preferences, skills gaps, and career path expectations.

strategic.label: Internal Input
strategic.tooltip: Gather internal stakeholder input - certification priorities, resource constraints.

strategic.label: Trend Analysis
strategic.tooltip: Where technology and job roles are heading - informs track roadmap.

strategic.label: Product Lifecycle Awareness
strategic.tooltip: Where product is in lifecycle - affects exam longevity planning.

strategic.label: Training-Certification Alignment
strategic.tooltip: Ensure training content is aligned to blueprint objectives.

tactical.label: Candidate Profile
tactical.tooltip: Minimally qualified candidate definition - anchor for standard setting.

tactical.label: SME Interviews
tactical.tooltip: Interviews with practicing professionals - structured interviews to understand job task requirements.

tactical.label: Retirement / Revision Strategy
tactical.tooltip: How the exam will be updated, replaced, or retired as technology changes.

tactical.label: Recertification Plan
tactical.tooltip: When candidates must recertify/renew to maintain their credential (cadence and criteria). Also covers the operational processing layer: how CEU submissions are received and verified, who audits continuing competency claims, and what happens when a candidate fails to renew (grace periods, lapsed status, reinstatement pathway). ISO/IEC 17024 Section 7.4 requires documented maintenance-of-certification procedures. Design the policy and the processing workflow together.

tactical.label: Identified Legal Policies
tactical.tooltip: Known legal requirements: NDAs, IP ownership, accessibility obligations, and testing regulations.

strategic.label: Assessment Methodology
strategic.tooltip: Select assessment methodology - form-based, adaptive (CAT), performance-based (PBT), or hybrid.

strategic.label: Psychometrician Engagement
strategic.tooltip: Engage and contract external psychometrician (where applicable).

strategic.label: Delivery Vendor Selection
strategic.tooltip: Select and contract exam delivery and proctoring vendor(s) - (Pearson VUE, Prometric, PSI, Kryterion, Examity etc.).

strategic.label: Item Banking Platform Selection
strategic.tooltip: Choosing the right item banking tool is a strategic exam planning decision (Certiverse, Alpine, Questionmark etc.), distinct from delivery vendor selection.

strategic.label: Credentialing Architecture / Platform
strategic.tooltip: Decide how credentials will be issued, displayed, and verified. Evaluate managed platforms (e.g., Alpine Testing's CM Connect) vs. self-hosted infrastructure.

strategic.label: Compliance & Accessibility
strategic.tooltip: ADA/Accessibility compliance plan, GDPR/Data Privacy for global candidates, and legal defensibility framework.

tactical.label: SME Recruitment & Onboarding
tactical.tooltip: Recruit, screen, and onboard SMEs. Manage diversity of job role, geography, and employer type.

tactical.label: NDA & Agreement Tracking
tactical.tooltip: Which SMEs have signed NDAs, completed training, and are cleared for workshop participation.

tactical.label: Conflict of Interest Management
tactical.tooltip: Conflicts of interest - Identify and document (training SMEs separated from exam development SMEs)

tactical.label: SME Performance Evaluation
tactical.tooltip: Evaluating SME contributions on collaboration, quality, efficiency, expertise and exam data etc..

tactical.label: SME Appreciation & Retention
tactical.tooltip: Social recognition, letters of recommendation, endorsements, and program outcome updates.

tactical.label: Workshop Facilitation
tactical.tooltip: Schedule, facilitate, and document JTA, IDW, and standard setting workshops.

strategic.label: SME Pool Strategy
strategic.tooltip: Define size, composition, and diversity targets for SME pool - establish ongoing pipeline of qualified contributors.

strategic.label: SME Value Proposition
strategic.tooltip: Develop sustainable SME community with clear value proposition.

strategic.label: SME Training Design
strategic.tooltip: Create SME curriculum training - standards for item writing, bloom's taxonomy, bias awareness, legal obligations etc...

strategic.label: Certification SME Advisory Board
strategic.tooltip: Standing body of senior SMEs provide ongoing program direction, external credibility to credential, and retention/recognition vehicle for top contributors. Different from workshop participants. Could also serve as your program evangelists.

tactical.label: Item Banking Platform Management
tactical.tooltip: The day-to-day administration of the item banking tool (user access, item workflows, version control, vendor relationship).

tactical.label: Job Task Analysis (JTA)
tactical.tooltip: Job Task Analysis workshop - documents KSAs for the job role. Output: blueprint, objectives, and candidate definitions.

tactical.label: Exam Blueprints
tactical.tooltip: Detailed outline of exam content: objectives, weightings, section structure.

tactical.label: Item Writing
tactical.tooltip: Exam questions created by SMEs, reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and difficulty.

tactical.label: Assessment Translation & Localization
tactical.tooltip: Translating exam content into other languages and adapting for regional terminology, cultural context, and locally relevant examples. Define best practices: translation, back-translation verification, and bilingual SME cultural review.

tactical.label: Beta Testing & Analysis
tactical.tooltip: Beta exam with real candidates - statistical analysis of item performance before scoring becomes operational. Could also publish an Operational Beta as an option.

tactical.label: Standard Setting
tactical.tooltip: Psychometrician and SMEs determine pass/fail threshold and number of exam forms.

tactical.label: Forms and Scoring
tactical.tooltip: Final determination of scoring model and number of exam versions/forms for operational delivery.

tactical.label: Exam Delivery
tactical.tooltip: Assembly of exam in delivery platform and quality assurance.

tactical.label: Exam Security Management
tactical.tooltip: Design scenario-based questions as security strategy (harder to memorize), set vendor flags for suspicious activity (e.g. completion time, score spikes), country blacklist, refresh exam content silently so brain-dump sites fall behind.

strategic.label: Item Format Strategy
strategic.tooltip: Item format should match the cognitive level required. E.g., scenario-based items deliver higher construct validity and are significantly harder to brain-dump than standalone MC items.

strategic.label: Passing Score Philosophy
strategic.tooltip: Some common standard-setting methods: Modified Angoff, Bookmark, Contrasting Groups. The Minimally Qualified Candidate (MQC) definition must be written and agreed before the standard setting workshop.

strategic.label: Item Bank Architecture
strategic.tooltip: Define how item pool will be maintained, equated, and updated across exam lifecycle.

tactical.label: Monitor Customer Feedback
tactical.tooltip: Candidate feedback on exam difficulty, content relevance, testing experience.

tactical.label: Monitor Track Performance
tactical.tooltip: Performance of each track against enrollment and pass rate targets.

tactical.label: Item Performance Data
tactical.tooltip: Item-level data - difficulty index, discrimination index, distractor analysis.

tactical.label: Pass Rate & Score Distribution
tactical.tooltip: Track the pass rate and score distribution analysis of each form as well as drift across forms and versions.

tactical.label: Candidate Demographics
tactical.tooltip: Candidate demographics - geography, employer type, job role, retakes.

tactical.label: Candidate Satisfaction Data
tactical.tooltip: Candidate satisfaction data - NPS, post-exam surveys, community sentiment - capture and analyze.

tactical.label: Psychometric Health Review
tactical.tooltip: Regular psychometric health check - statistical review of item performance across forms.

tactical.label: In-Service Analysis
tactical.tooltip: Statistical review conducted post-launch prior to full psychometric health check. Validates top performers score high, novices score low, borderline candidates cluster near cut score. Confirms exam measures correctly.

strategic.label: Identify Add-on Tracks
strategic.tooltip: Opportunities to extend certification into adjacent tracks or specializations.

strategic.label: Determine Marketing Needs
strategic.tooltip: Additional marketing investment needed - determine where to focus.

strategic.label: Product / Cur. Lifecycle Monitoring
strategic.tooltip: Monitoring product and curriculum lifecycle to anticipate when exam updates will be required.

strategic.label: Analytics Architecture
strategic.tooltip: Define what candidate, item, and program data is collected; how it flows; who has access at each stage. ISO/IEC 17024 Section 8 requires records management procedures. GDPR Article 30 requires a Record of Processing Activities (ROPA) if you process EU candidate data. Data minimization: collect only what is needed for psychometric validity and program improvement. Establish a retention schedule.

strategic.label: Program KPI Framework
strategic.tooltip: Define KPIs and OKRs for the certification program.

strategic.label: ROI Modeling
strategic.tooltip: ROI of certification program using data - candidate earning uplift, partner revenue correlation etc...

tactical.label: Datasheets
tactical.tooltip: Product fact sheets and certification program datasheets for candidates.

tactical.label: Delivery Platform Updates
tactical.tooltip: Always keep exam delivery platform pages, scheduling links, and pricing current.

tactical.label: Presentations / Web Events
tactical.tooltip: Promotion through webinars/virtual, and conference events.

tactical.label: Community Updates
tactical.tooltip: Update certification communities, user groups, social channels.

tactical.label: Segmented Emails
tactical.tooltip: Email campaigns targeted to candidate segments by role, level, product area, region etc..

tactical.label: Branded UI Updates
tactical.tooltip: Branded exam UI, and candidate-facing interfaces.

tactical.label: Partner Channel Comms
tactical.tooltip: Communication strategy for authorized training providers, resellers, channel partners.

tactical.label: FAQ & Call Deflection
tactical.tooltip: Searchable FAQ as first line of support for candidates - comprehensive, searchable. Update FAQs with every recurring support inquiry. Your website is call deflection center - easy to find, easy to use, always current.

tactical.label: Credentialed Professional Registry
tactical.tooltip: Public directory of certified individuals where candidates can share and employers can verify. Supports ecosystem proof points, supports recruiter discovery, provides certified professionals with shareable profile artifact.

strategic.label: Identify Audiences
strategic.tooltip: Identify and segment audiences who should receive different messaging.

strategic.label: Product Positioning
strategic.tooltip: Position certification relative to competitors and alternatives.

strategic.label: Marketing Coordination
strategic.tooltip: Coordinate with marketing on integrated campaigns and content calendar.

strategic.label: Employer Adoption Strategy
strategic.tooltip: Strategy for employer and partner adoption - how do you get companies to require/recognize certification?

strategic.label: Demand Generation Strategy
strategic.tooltip: Push-pull demand generation, subscription programs for training revenue forecasting, salary survey participation, advertising in industry publications, or test prep offerings that pull candidates into funnel rather than losing those candidates to brain dump sites.

tactical.label: Instructor Reference Guides
tactical.tooltip: Instructor/trainers supporting certification candidates.

tactical.label: Internal Presentations
tactical.tooltip: Presentations for internal stakeholders regarding program status and results.

tactical.label: Internal Release Announcements
tactical.tooltip: Notices to internal teams regarding new exams/tracks released.

tactical.label: Internal Site Updates
tactical.tooltip: Internal knowledge base, intranet pages, team sites - keep current.

tactical.label: Training Partners / ATC Enablement
tactical.tooltip: Onboarding and enablement content for Authorized Training Centers.

strategic.label: Project Justification / Approval
strategic.tooltip: Business case and secure budget/resources approval for new certification initiatives.

strategic.label: Build Key Contacts
strategic.tooltip: Identify key internal and external stakeholders who need to be informed and involved. Formalize the stakeholder engagement in your program governance.

strategic.label: Executive Steering & OKRs
strategic.tooltip: Cross-functionally review program health, OKRs, and strategic direction regularly via scheduled executive meetings.

tactical.label: CS & Fulfillment Readiness
tactical.tooltip: Verify that all customer service and examination fulfillment processes are able to handle the expected number of candidates.

tactical.label: Measurement & Feedback
tactical.tooltip: Use candidate and stakeholder feedback to guide actions after launch (i.e., post-launch).

tactical.label: Delivery Readiness
tactical.tooltip: Verify that all delivery infrastructure has been put into production and tested in a complete end-to-end fashion prior to launch.

tactical.label: Sales Readiness
tactical.tooltip: Prepares sales teams with messaging on competitive position and value propositions of certification.

tactical.label: Candidate Experience Audit
tactical.tooltip: Reviews candidate experience post-launch (registration process, score reporting, issuing badges etc..)

tactical.label: Digital Badge Readiness
tactical.tooltip: Verifies that badging is live, the data used to create the badge is correct and issuance is triggered.

tactical.label: Credentialing Platform Readiness
tactical.tooltip: Verifies the certification logic is correct and displaying the credentials as per.

tactical.label: Delivery & Badging Platform Management
tactical.tooltip: The ongoing post-launch management of the exam delivery platform and badge issuance pipeline, separate from initial readiness checks.

tactical.label: Exam Launch Checklist
tactical.tooltip: A per-exam Go-Live Checklist addressing operational readiness, alerting stakeholders, promoting marketing initiatives, updating FAQs, and sending company-wide communications. This is separate from the gateways related to development, it will be the operational launch sign-off for each new individual exam.

strategic.label: Candidate Journey Design
strategic.tooltip: Identify the entire candidate experience from awareness through credential display (post-cert experience, renewal path).

strategic.label: Recertification Experience
strategic.tooltip: Define the recertification candidate experience (what does renewing involve?); Design continuous education pathways for candidates.

tactical.label: Help Desk & Ticket Management
tactical.tooltip: Handle incoming candidate inquiries on exam scheduling, score reports, voucher redemption, and platform access. First line of resolution before escalation.

tactical.label: ADA / Accommodation Request Processing
tactical.tooltip: Review, approve, and coordinate accommodation requests for candidates with disabilities. Liaise with your delivery vendors to ensure accommodations are correctly applied at the testing center or remote session.

tactical.label: Disputes & Appeals Management
tactical.tooltip: Process structured appeals where candidates contest exam fairness, content accuracy, or scoring. Manage within defined policy timelines and escalation paths with independent review.

tactical.label: Exam Incident Management
tactical.tooltip: Handle technical failures during exam delivery including system crashes, proctor issues, connectivity problems, and test center incidents. Coordinate resolution with delivery vendor and determine candidate remedy.

tactical.label: Cheating & Misconduct Investigations
tactical.tooltip: Investigate flagged candidates based on unusual score patterns, completion time anomalies, proxy testing suspicions, or confirmed brain dump use. Document findings and apply resolution framework.

tactical.label: Refund & Reschedule Management
tactical.tooltip: Process exam cancellations, reschedule requests, no-shows, and refund eligibility determinations in accordance with program policy.

tactical.label: Badge & Certificate Issuance Support
tactical.tooltip: Resolve cases where candidates have not received or cannot access their digital badge or certificate after completing all requirements. Coordinate with badging and credentialing platforms.

tactical.label: Eligibility & Prerequisites Support
tactical.tooltip: Answer candidate questions about exam eligibility, prerequisites, and registration requirements. Reduce ticket volume by ensuring this information is clearly published and searchable.

tactical.label: Credentialing Applications Processing
tactical.tooltip: The ongoing operational function of processing eligibility applications, verifying candidate qualifications against program requirements, making eligibility determinations, and formally awarding credentials post-exam. Distinct from Eligibility & Prerequisites Support (reactive Q&A); which is the back-office processing operation. You must have an audit trail for each eligibility determination and credential award decision.

strategic.label: Support Policy Design
strategic.tooltip: Define the policies governing appeals, refunds, accommodations, and misconduct including timelines, decision authority, and candidate communication standards.

strategic.label: Cheating Resolution Framework
strategic.tooltip: Define the end-to-end process for investigating misconduct allegations, the evidence threshold for action, the range of consequences, and the appeals rights of the accused candidate.

strategic.label: ADA / Accessibility Policy
strategic.tooltip: Define what accommodations are offered, eligibility and documentation criteria, approval authority, and vendor compliance requirements. Ensure alignment with ADA and applicable regional regulations.

strategic.label: SLA Definition & Monitoring
strategic.tooltip: Set response time standards across support tiers (first response, resolution, escalation). Monitor adherence and use SLA data to identify staffing or process gaps.

strategic.label: Support Platform Selection
strategic.tooltip: Select and manage the ticketing or CRM platform used for candidate support operations. Define workflows, tagging taxonomy, and integration with the candidate database.

strategic.label: Escalation Framework
strategic.tooltip: Define when and how issues escalate beyond frontline support including who has authority to make final decisions on disputes, misconduct rulings, and policy exceptions.

strategic.label: Support Analytics & Feedback Loop
strategic.tooltip: Track ticket volume, resolution times, and recurring issue types. Use data to identify program gaps, improve self-service content, and feed findings back into governance and exam planning and support documentation.

tactical.label: Certified Team Compliance Tracking
tactical.tooltip: Monitor the % and count of each partner organization. Flag partners below the threshold and trigger outreach or remediation plans.

tactical.label: Partner Program Tier Management
tactical.tooltip: Manage partner tier status based on certification achievements including processing upgrades, downgrades, and notifying partners of status changes that affect program benefits.

tactical.label: Partner Voucher Management
tactical.tooltip: Distribute exam vouchers to partners, manage redemption, track consumption by partner organization and role. Reconcile against partner tier entitlements.

tactical.label: Partner Onboarding & Enablement
tactical.tooltip: Onboard new partners to the certification program.

tactical.label: Partner Channel Comms
tactical.tooltip: Regular comms to the partner network: new exam launches, blueprint updates, pricing changes, program incentives, and certification news. Differentiate messaging by partner tier.

tactical.label: Partner NPS & Satisfaction Monitoring
tactical.tooltip: Track partner NPS, post-exam satisfaction scores, and partner feedback on the certification program. Use data to improve the partner experience.

tactical.label: RPCI & Deal Impact Tracking
tactical.tooltip: Monitor Revenue Per Certified Individual (RPCI) by partner organization. Use this as a leading indicator: IDC research shows more certified staff = greater revenue per certified individual.

strategic.label: Partner Value Proposition
strategic.tooltip: Define and document the business case for partner certification. IDC: fully certified teams are 35% more likely to finish ahead of schedule, meet 95% of client objectives, and score 36% higher NPS than partially certified teams.

strategic.label: Partner Program Architecture
strategic.tooltip: Design the partner certification program structure: tier levels (e.g. Authorized, Premier, Elite), certification requirements per tier per role, and progression pathways for partner organizations.

strategic.label: Channel Adoption & Incentive Strategy
strategic.tooltip: Define the incentive strategy to drive partner certification adoption: tier benefits, deal registration advantages, MDF eligibility, co-marketing rights, and recognition programs etc.

strategic.label: Partner Skill Requirements by Role
strategic.tooltip: Define which certifications are required for each partner role (Architect, Admin, Developer, Sales Engineer, etc.) aligned to the partner's engagement model.

strategic.label: Partner ROI Framework
strategic.tooltip: Build the ROI model for the partner program e.g., using Revenue Per Certified Individual (RPCI) data, deal size correlation, project success rates, and customer satisfaction metrics. Use to make the internal business case and to market the program to partners.

strategic.label: Partner Demand Generation Strategy
strategic.tooltip: Strategy to generate demand from partner organizations for certification: executive briefings, case study data, salary/revenue uplift data, and recognition programs that create internal partner pressure to certify.

tactical.label: Application Completion Monitoring
tactical.tooltip: Track where candidates drop off in the application and scheduling funnel. Application completion rate is a primary leading indicator of funnel health. Flag stalled registrations and trigger re-engagement outreach before candidates go cold.

tactical.label: Candidate Onboarding Comms
tactical.tooltip: Welcome sequence for newly registered candidates: confirmation, scheduling guidance, study resource links, and exam-day expectations. A structured onboarding sequence directly reduces no-shows and early-stage dropout.

tactical.label: Behavioral Trigger Messaging
tactical.tooltip: Automated messages triggered by candidate actions or inaction: nudges for unfinished applications, encouragement for candidates who did not pass, reminders before voucher or eligibility expiry. Alpine Testing's CM Connect and similar platforms support trigger-based delivery tied to candidate status changes.

tactical.label: Study Support & Prep
tactical.tooltip: Provide practice exams, exam guides, and pre-exam check-ins to reduce candidate anxiety and dropout. Study support at the right moments directly impacts pass rates and NPS. Candidates who feel supported are significantly more likely to complete and recommend the program.

tactical.label: Post-Exam Follow-Up
tactical.tooltip: Structured outreach after every exam outcome: congratulations + badge/certificate guidance; empathetic retake pathway with resources for candidates who did not pass.

tactical.label: Multi-Channel Outreach
tactical.tooltip: Coordinate candidate communications across email, SMS, and in-platform messaging. Channel selection should match message urgency and candidate preference data. All time-sensitive messages (exam reminders, expiry warnings) warrant SMS; content and study support suit email. Be mindful of the message intent; transactional vs. marketing.

tactical.label: Renewal & Recertification Nudges
tactical.tooltip: Proactive outreach to credentialed professionals approaching expiry: renewal reminders, continuing education options, and lapsed-credential re-engagement campaigns. Renewal outreach is lower cost than acquiring a new candidate so treat it as a retention program, not an admin task.

strategic.label: Candidate Engagement Strategy
strategic.tooltip: Define the overall engagement model: which lifecycle stages require touchpoints, what outcome each touchpoint drives, and how engagement KPIs connect to program health metrics. Proactive candidate engagement reduces dropout and increases renewal rates. Do not fall into the reactive ad hoc mode.

strategic.label: Engagement Platform Selection
strategic.tooltip: Select and configure the candidate engagement and CRM platform (or custom CRM integration). Define data flows between delivery vendor, item bank, and engagement platform. The platform must support behavioral triggers, segmentation, and multi-channel delivery to be effective.

strategic.label: Personalization Framework
strategic.tooltip: Define the rules engine for personalized content delivery: what triggers which message, to which segment, through which channel. Moves communications from generic blasts to behavior-driven sequences.

strategic.label: Post-Certification Community Strategy
strategic.tooltip: Strategy for keeping credentialed professionals engaged after certification: alumni communities, exclusive content, recognition programs, and advocacy pathways. Certifyd.co offers physical certificates for candidates to display in real life. Converts certified professionals into your evangelists and recurring exam candidates. Post-certification engagement is the lowest-cost demand generation lever available and credentialed alumni are your highest-trust marketing channel.
